solution: ```
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def removeZeroSumSublists(self, head: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        dummy = ListNode(0, head)
        prefix = 0
        seen = {}
        cur = dummy
        while cur:
            prefix += cur.val
            seen[prefix] = cur
            cur = cur.next
        
        prefix = 0
        cur = dummy
        while cur:
            prefix += cur.val
            cur.next = seen[prefix].next
            cur = cur.next
        
        return dummy.next
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N)
space complexity: O(N)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses prefix sums to detect zero-sum ranges in one pass, storing the last occurrence of each prefix. A second pass splices out ranges whose start and end share the same prefix sum, achieving linear time and space.
